task_signature.context: Test types, counts, and patterns for FitMe features. Derived from Home v2 (21 tests: 11 functional + 10 analytics) and Training v2 (16 tests: unit + integration). Used by /qa skill in Phase 5.

learned_patterns.pattern: Analytics tests — XCTest event verification (highest priority)
learned_patterns.decision: Analytics tests verify: (1) event fires on the correct user action, (2) event has correct name (screen-prefixed), (3) event has correct parameters and values, (4) event does NOT fire when consent is denied (consent gating). Home v2 added 10 analytics tests (all 10 passed). Test names pattern: testHomeActionTapEventFires(), testHomeEmptyStateShownOnNoData(), testAnalyticsEventBlockedWithoutConsent().
learned_patterns.typical_count: 3-4 analytics tests per event, scaled by event count. Home v2: 3 events → 10 tests. Training v2: 12 events → proportional.

test_structure.given: Set up MockAnalyticsService with event recording
test_structure.when: Trigger the user action (tap, navigation, state change)
test_structure.then: Assert MockAnalyticsService.capturedEvents.last?.name == 'home_action_tap' AND params match AND consent gate respected

learned_patterns.pattern: Functional/behavior tests — service layer and logic
learned_patterns.decision: XCTest unit tests for service classes and recommendation providers. Home v2 T15: behavior tests for HomeRecommendationProvider (logic: which action to surface, empty state trigger, ordering). Training v2: unit + integration tests for exercise state management, set completion, rest timer logic. Test count: typically 8-15 per service class.
learned_patterns.typical_count: Home v2: 11 functional tests total. Training v2: 16 tests (mix of unit + integration).

test_categories.service_unit_tests: Pure logic, no UI. Test HomeRecommendationProvider.recommendedAction(for:), TrainingSession state machine transitions.
test_categories.integration_tests: Service + ViewModel interaction. Verify ViewModel.load() triggers correct provider calls and state transitions.
test_categories.state_machine_tests: Empty → Loading → Loaded → Error transitions. Each state gets at least one test.

learned_patterns.pattern: Snapshot tests — deferred or Phase 5 manual verification
learned_patterns.decision: Snapshot tests for 5 UI states x device sizes. Home v2 T16 (snapshot tests) was deferred from Phase 4 to Phase 5 manual verification. States to cover: (1) full data / happy path, (2) empty state, (3) loading/skeleton, (4) error state, (5) partial data. Devices: iPhone SE (375pt), iPhone 17 Pro (393pt), iPhone 17 Pro Max (430pt).
learned_patterns.typical_count: 5 states x 3 devices = 15 snapshot assertions minimum.
learned_patterns.gotcha: Snapshot tests are fragile to font/color rendering differences across OS versions. Pin simulator to specific iOS version in CI. Home v2 deferred T16 precisely because of CI snapshot flakiness risk.
source_executions: home-today-screen tasks T16: 'Write snapshot tests for v2 view (5 states x devices)' — deferred to Phase 5
home-today-screen state.json testing.ci_passed = true (without snapshot tests)

learned_patterns.pattern: CI gate — three required checks before merge
learned_patterns.decision: All three must pass before Phase 6 (Review) approval: (1) make tokens-check — design system drift detection; (2) xcodebuild build — iOS Simulator, no code signing, must produce BUILD SUCCEEDED; (3) xcodebuild test — XCTest suite. Home v2 build result: 'BUILD SUCCEEDED (iPhone 17 Pro, iOS 26.4)'. CI must pass on BOTH the feature branch AND main.
source_executions: home-today-screen state.json testing.ci_passed = true, build_result = 'BUILD SUCCEEDED (iPhone 17 Pro, iOS 26.4)'
home-today-screen state.json review.ci_main = true, ci_feature = true

learned_patterns.pattern: Accessibility test coverage — required for v2 refactors
learned_patterns.decision: AX/Accessibility pass is a separate implementation task (Home v2 T11: 'Accessibility pass on v2 view (~30+ labels)'). Testing verifies: accessibilityLabel on all interactive elements, accessibilityHint on non-obvious actions, accessibilityIdentifier on testable elements, VoiceOver traversal order. Minimum 30 labels for complex screens (Home, Training). Training v2: Full AX5 Dynamic Type support required.
learned_patterns.typical_count: 30+ accessibilityLabel declarations for complex screens. Verify in XCTest via accessibility inspector or XCUITest accessibilityElement queries.
source_executions: home-today-screen T11 '~30+ labels', training-plan-v2 Q6_dynamic_type = 'Full AX5 support'

learned_patterns.pattern: Test file naming and organization conventions
learned_patterns.decision: Test files mirror the source file structure. HomeRecommendationProvider.swift → HomeRecommendationProviderTests.swift. Analytics tests go in a separate test file: HomeAnalyticsTests.swift, TrainingAnalyticsTests.swift. Training v2 decomposition: 6 extracted views each get their own test consideration. analytics_tests separate from functional tests for clear separation of concerns.
source_executions: home-today-screen T15 (HomeRecommendationProvider tests), T17 (analytics tests separate task)

learned_patterns.decision: Walking Sections A-K of v2-refactor-checklist.md is itself a test task (Home v2 T14). This is done AFTER T8 (main assembly) + T11 (a11y pass) + T12 (pbxproj). state.json phases.implementation.checklist_completed must be true. Checklist sections: A (audit/spec), B (file convention), C (token compliance), D (component reuse), E (state coverage), F (accessibility), G (motion), H (analytics), I (pbxproj hygiene), J (CI), K (documentation).
source_executions: home-today-screen T14: 'Walk v2 refactor checklist (Sections A-K)'

anti_patterns.pattern: Writing tests after merge instead of during Phase 5
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Tests written post-merge miss the CI gate requirement. Both branches (feature + main) must be green BEFORE merge approval. Tests are Phase 5 work, not cleanup.
anti_patterns.source_execution: CLAUDE.md CI requirement: both branches must pass before merge is approved

anti_patterns.pattern: Analytics tests that only check event name, not parameters
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Incomplete analytics tests pass when event fires with wrong parameters (e.g. weight in pounds instead of kg, missing exercise_name). Always assert BOTH the event name AND all expected parameters. Consent gating test is also mandatory.
anti_patterns.source_execution: home-today-screen state.json testing.analytics_verification_passed = true — requires full verification

anti_patterns.pattern: Running make tokens-check last instead of first
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: If tokens-check fails, it means raw literals exist in the v2 file — this indicates a deeper problem that may require refactoring multiple views. Catch it early by running tokens-check after T1 (token additions) before T8 (main assembly).
anti_patterns.source_execution: v2-refactor-checklist B4 — run tokens-check to verify no drift

speedup_instructions: When /qa plans a test strategy for a v2 refactor: (1) analytics tests first (highest ROI — verify event names match taxonomy CSV), (2) service/provider unit tests (behavior tests for any new service classes), (3) CI gate validation (tokens-check + build + test suite), (4) checklist walk (T14 equivalent). Use test counts from Home v2 (21 total: 11 functional + 10 analytics) as the baseline for similar-complexity screens. Defer snapshot tests to Phase 5 if CI schedule is tight — they're valuable but fragile.
